title: Question library + project-type taxonomy + PROJECT.md schema/writer
description:
Second ticket in the project-intake wizard chain (TASK-010..015). Builds on `src/question-engine.js` (TASK-010) to deliver the actual question catalog the wizard will ask, the curated project-type taxonomy locked in the 2026-05-26 design conversation, and the PROJECT.md persistence layer that captures the answers.

Deliverables:

1. **`src/question-library.js`** — exports `COMMON_QUESTIONS` (≥6 questions covering project name, description, type, target_users, success_criteria, primary_use_cases) and `TYPE_SPECIFIC_QUESTIONS` (an object keyed by project_type values, each value an array of questions gated by `when: a => a.project_type === '<type>'`). Also exports a helper that returns the full combined array suitable for one `runQuestionnaire` invocation.

2. **Project-type taxonomy** locked as the enum `['web-saas', 'cli-tool', 'data-pipeline', 'ml-model', 'library', 'other']`. Every type except 'other' has ≥3 type-specific questions; 'other' has 1 free-text question capturing the user's own description (LLM inference happens downstream in TASK-013).

3. **`src/project-md.js`** — exports `writeProjectMd({repoRoot, answers, now})` and `readProjectMd({repoRoot})`. PROJECT.md lives at `<repoRoot>/PROJECT.md`. Format: minimal YAML frontmatter (name, type, created_at, schema_version) + markdown body with prose sections per answer. Round-trip lossless: write then read returns the same answers map.

4. **`state/PROJECT.schema.json`** — JSON Schema for the frontmatter shape. `schema_version: 1` to enable future migration.

5. **No external YAML dep.** Constrain the frontmatter to a subset (string-or-array-of-strings scalars) so we can write a 20-line in-house parser. Avoids dragging in js-yaml or gray-matter.

All writes go through `atomicWriteFile` from src/atomic-write.js. Question definitions reuse the engine's declarative shape; `when` predicates handle the project_type branching.
acceptance_criteria: `src/question-library.js` exports `COMMON_QUESTIONS` (≥6 entries) and `TYPE_SPECIFIC_QUESTIONS` (object keyed by project_type values). A helper `buildIntakeQuestions()` returns the concatenated array ready for `runQuestionnaire`.
The `project_type` enum is exactly `['web-saas', 'cli-tool', 'data-pipeline', 'ml-model', 'library', 'other']` — verified by a test that asserts the literal array contents.

Every project_type except 'other' has ≥3 type-specific questions, each gated by `when: a => a.project_type === '<type>'`; the 'other' branch has exactly 1 free-text question for architecture description.

`src/project-md.js` exports `writeProjectMd({repoRoot, answers, now})` and `readProjectMd({repoRoot})`. Round-trip test: `writeProjectMd(answers)` then `readProjectMd()` returns an equivalent answers map (same keys, same values).
`writeProjectMd` uses `atomicWriteFile` from src/atomic-write.js — verified via `vi.mock('node:fs')` and the same fsync-before-rename invariant used in TASK-001 and TASK-010.
PROJECT.md format is documented in src/project-md.js with a working example and parses with the in-house minimal parser (no external dep added to package.json).
`state/PROJECT.schema.json` exists, declares `schema_version: 1` as required, and validates a freshly written PROJECT.md frontmatter via ajv (same pattern as `tests/live-state.spec.js`).

End-to-end test: run `runQuestionnaire(buildIntakeQuestions())` with a fake prompter scripted for the 'web-saas' branch, then `writeProjectMd(answers)`, then `readProjectMd`, then ajv-validate frontmatter. All steps succeed in one test.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created. Design fixed pre-tests: taxonomy enum locked as [web-saas, cli-tool, data-pipeline, ml-model, library, other]; questions are declarative (engine-compatible shape from TASK-010); type-specific branching uses `when: a => a.project_type === 'X'` so the whole intake runs in one engine invocation; PROJECT.md uses minimal YAML frontmatter (no external dep, in-house ~20-line parser constrained to string-or-array-of-strings scalars) + markdown body for prose. Workflow: developer writes failing Vitest suite covering all 8 ACs, then implements src/question-library.js + src/project-md.js + state/PROJECT.schema.json, then reviewer in fresh context. No bin/init.js wiring in this ticket — that's TASK-012.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body:
Closed. Workflow: tests-first commit `339d871` (16 failing specs across 4 new test files; phase-1 dev surfaced the `language` id collision between cli-tool and library branches as a real blocker pre-impl) + impl commit `80972d9` (src/question-library.js + src/project-md.js + state/PROJECT.schema.json; per-branch namespacing applied to both `language` -> {cli_language, library_language} and `deployment_target` -> {web_deployment_target, ml_deployment_target}; in-house YAML parser constrained to scalar `key: value` + inline `key: [a, b, c]` arrays with nested-object rejection; single `BODY_SECTIONS` constant consumed by both writer and reader as the source-of-truth for the id↔section mapping; impl commit body documented all three decisions per the TASK-010 reviewer's lesson). Reviewer in fresh context returned **APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS** (0 HIGH, 1 MEDIUM, 4 LOW, 2 NIT). 90/90 vitest specs green in ~9.5s, atomic-write invariant verified, no external YAML dep added (package.json diff empty), schema validates a freshly-written PROJECT.md via ajv2020.

AC coverage map: AC1 (COMMON_QUESTIONS ≥6 + TYPE_SPECIFIC_QUESTIONS + buildIntakeQuestions) — `common_questions_min_six`, `build_intake_questions_concatenates`; AC2 (taxonomy lock) — `taxonomy_enum_is_locked`; AC3 (≥3 per type, 'other'==1, when predicates) — `each_type_has_at_least_three_questions`, `when_predicate_gates_correctly`; AC4 (round-trip) — `round_trip_preserves_string_answers`, `round_trip_preserves_array_answers`, `readProjectMd_throws_clear_error_on_missing_file`; AC5 (atomic write) — `writeProjectMd_uses_atomic_write`; AC6 (in-house parser, no dep) — `in_house_parser_rejects_unsupported_yaml`, `frontmatter_uses_explicit_delimiters`; AC7 (schema validation) — `schema_validates_freshly_written_frontmatter`, `schema_requires_schema_version_field`, `schema_rejects_unknown_project_type`; AC8 (e2e) — `end_to_end_intake_for_web_saas`.

Reviewer findings rolled forward to TASK-016 (PROJECT.md hardening): MEDIUM (comma-in-array-item silent corruption — not reachable from wizard happy path today but a hazard once TASK-013 lands a non-wizard caller of writeProjectMd) + 4 LOWs (coerceFrontmatterScalar integer-string coercion risk for future fields, stack-value coercion asymmetry, silent default-empty-string fallback for missing project_name/project_type, comment misleading about colons in values). 2 NITs noted in-line. Question-library final counts: web-saas 4, cli-tool 3, data-pipeline 3, ml-model 4, library 3, other 1 — all branches meet the spec.

Design choice respected: phase-2 dev kept stack bullets in snake_case rather than humanizing (e.g. `- frontend_framework: react` not `- Frontend framework: React`). Defensible — humanizing+dehumanizing is irreversible for round-trip; TASK-015 (UX polish) can add a separate `label` field per question if pretty labels are wanted in PROJECT.md.
